exemplar.title: An investigation into the effect of anchoring bias on decision-making

detailedExemplarData.title: An investigation into the effect of anchoring bias on decision-making

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear statement of investigation aim with relevance to anchoring bias and dualâprocess theory
Accurate description of Dual Process Theory and anchoring heuristic linked to manipulation
Fully operationalised hypotheses with explicit directional wording and correctly identified variables
weaknesses: Original study description is truncated and lacks full procedural detail
Theoretical context could be deepened by contrasting with competing models or heuristics
Minor typographical and formatting inconsistencies in hypothesis presentation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction meets the band 5â6 descriptors across all strands: the aim is clearly stated and its relevance explained, theory and model are accurately described and linked, and variables are fully operationalised with hypotheses. Minor omissions (cutâoff description, limited theoretical depth, formatting issues) preclude the very top mark. A holistic score of 5 reflects strong performance with room for refinement.

strengths: Independentâmeasures design is not only described but explained with respect to internal validity considerations
Opportunity sampling technique and participant characteristics are given, showing awareness of generalisability issues
Control variables (time limits, seating separation) and materials are listed with initial justification
weaknesses: Sampling rationale is abruptly cut off and lacks full explanation
Materials list is not fully justified (e.g., role of participantâsupplied pencils)
Ethical debrief procedures are mentioned but not adequately detailed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the exploration section demonstrates clear understanding of design, sampling, and controls, key justifications are incomplete or truncated. The sampling rationale ends abruptly, materials are not fully explained, and debriefing details are lacking. These gaps warrant a modest reduction from the top mark, yielding a score of 3.

strengths: Appropriate use of descriptive (mean, SD) and inferential (MannâWhitney U) statistics consistent with hypotheses
Graphical presentation (bar graph with error bars) correctly addresses the hypothesis
Interpretation links statistical findings to the directional hypothesis and discusses data dispersion
weaknesses: Minor uncertainty in the choice of criticalâvalue table and lack of effectâsize reporting
Graph axes lack numerical scales and clear tick labels
Transitional narrative from descriptive to inferential statistics could be smoother
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis robustly applies and interprets both descriptive and inferential statistics, and the graph addresses the hypothesis. Minor presentational and reporting issues prevent the very top mark. A score of 5 reflects largely accurate and appropriate statistical work with minor refinements needed.

strengths: Limitations are directly linked to justified modifications, demonstrating clear evaluative thinking
Findings are discussed with reference to Tversky & Kahnemanâs theory and Dual Process Theory
Strengths and limitations are relevant and explained in terms of validity and reliability
weaknesses: Discussion could acknowledge alternative cognitive biases or conflicting evidence to deepen nuance
Some modificationâlimitation links could be more explicitly stated
Evaluation lacks discussion of effectâsize implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation demonstrates strong linkage between findings, theory, limitations, and modifications. While modifications are wellâjustified, and strengths/limitations are explained, the discussion could be enriched with alternative perspectives and deeper nuance. A score of 5 appropriately balances these considerations.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar graph with error bars is provided, correctly comparing means across conditions and aligning with the hypothesis.  Axes are labelled, though units/scale detail is minimal.  Given this minor presentational shortfall, awarding 5 within the top band reflects strong but not flawless execution.

The student states the statistical outcome, interprets significance, and explicitly links findings back to the directional hypothesis.  Discussion of effect direction and reference to dispersion broadens the interpretation.  While insightful, it stops short of deeper effect-size commentary, so a 5 in the highest band is appropriate.

Both IV and DV are clearly stated and fully operationalised: IV = order of the factorial expression (1Ãâ¦8 vs. 8Ãâ¦1); DV = numerical estimate provided.  Separate null and one-tailed research hypotheses are given with explicit directional wording.  This exactly matches the top-band descriptor, so the full 6 marks within band 5-6 are justified.

Opportunity sampling is named, its convenience and potential bias are discussed, and participant characteristics (age, gender balance, cultural background, maths ability) are linked to generalisability.  Thus the choice of participants is explicitly explained, fulfilling the top of band 3-4; a score of 4 is appropriate.

Dual Process Theory and the anchoring heuristic are accurately described and explicitly connected to the planned manipulation (first number as anchor).  The description is coherent and error-free, but the theoretical depth is not outstanding (e.g., no competing explanations).  The work therefore meets band 5-6, with a solid but not exceptional performance meriting a 5.

Findings are related to Tversky & Kahneman and Dual Process Theory with a reasoned explanation of anchoring under System 1.  The discussion is clear but not particularly nuanced (e.g., lacks reference to conflicting evidence), so 5 within the top band reflects strong but improvable discussion.

The student explicitly states the aim â to replicate Tversky and Kahneman (1974) and examine whether order (ascending vs. descending) influences product estimates â and embeds this aim within a clear rationale about anchoring bias and everyday decision-making.  The explanation makes it clear why the replication is relevant to understanding heuristics and dual-process thinking, although it is not exceptionally detailed.  This comfortably fits the descriptor for band 5-6; a mid-band score of 5 is appropriate.

Multiple strengths and limitations (laboratory control, ecological validity, sampling biases, maths-ability confound) are explained and directly tied to validity and reliability, satisfying the âexplained and relevantâ requirement.  Some points remain surface-level, so a 5 rather than 6 is appropriate.

Key controls (5-second limit, seating separation, standardised instructions) are described and linked to minimising System-2 processing and cheating, showing clear explanation.  Materials are listed and briefly justified.  This aligns with the âexplainedâ descriptor in band 3-4, meriting 4 marks.

Each stated limitation is paired with a concrete, well-justified modification (naturalistic setting to raise ecological validity; matched-pairs design to control maths ability; varied time limits to examine System-1/2 balance).  The direct linkage and justification meet the descriptor for full marks, so a 6 within the top band is awarded.

The independent-measures design is not only described but also explained through comments on demand characteristics and internal validity.  Limitations of participant variability are acknowledged, demonstrating reasoning.  This meets the âexplainedâ criterion in band 3-4; the explanation is clear, so the higher mark (4) is warranted.

Appropriate descriptive (mean, SD) and inferential (Mann-Whitney U for two independent groups with non-parametric assumptions) statistics are applied.  Calculations appear internally consistent and the one-tailed p-value matches the stated hypothesis.  Minor uncertainty over critical-value table choice prevents full assurance of perfect accuracy, so a 5 (rather than 6) is prudent within the top band.

annotations.comment: The title clearly articulates the investigation topic and level, immediately orienting the reader to the focus on anchoring bias in decision-making.
Referencing Attwood et al. (2004) on ergonomics is not directly relevant to decision-making heuristics. Consider replacing with a cognitive psychology source focused on judgment under uncertainty.
The Dual Process Theory description is accurate but could be strengthened by briefly contrasting with competing models or other heuristics to deepen theoretical context.
While the anchoring bias definition is sound, you could explicitly link it to your research aim here to reinforce the connection between theory and your manipulation.
The sampling description ends abruptly and appears incomplete. Ensure the full rationale for opportunity sampling is provided.
Participant demographics (age range, gender balance, language proficiency) are thoroughly detailed, reinforcing the credibility of your sample description.
Control variables are justified with respect to System 1 processing and cheating prevention, showing clear methodological reasoning.
List your materials with brief justifications (e.g., why participant-supplied pencils could affect performance) to clarify their choice and role.
Expand on how your debrief (Appendix B) addresses potential deception, including timing and content, to strengthen ethical transparency.
Standard deviations are disproportionately large relative to means, suggesting possible outlier influence or calculation error. Re-check and consider trimming extreme values.
The caption for Table 1 is concise and informative, correctly identifying measures and conditions.
The description of Tversky & Kahnemanâs original study is cut off mid-sentence, leaving the descending condition incomplete. Ensure the full procedure is clearly stated.
This paragraph concisely restates the original studyâs method and findings, providing a solid rationale for replication.
When stating your replication aim, briefly explain how your investigation extends or differs from the original work to highlight its contribution.
The Independent Variable is clearly defined and operationalised as the order of the multiplication sequence.
The Dependent Variable is well operationalised as the numerical estimate provided under each condition.
Present the null hypothesis using LaTeX formatting (e.g., $$H_0$$) to maintain typographical consistency.
Typographical error: replace âconditionalâ with âconditionâ in the research hypothesis to ensure precision.
The independent-measures design is well explained, including its benefits for internal validity. This enhances the clarity of your research design.
The graph choice is appropriate, but axis scales and tick labels are missing. Add numerical scales to improve interpretability.
Interpretation of descriptive statistics explicitly links differences to the directional hypothesis, demonstrating strong integration of results and aim.
The transition to inferential statistics could be smoother. Consider adding a brief summary sentence bridging descriptive findings and test selection.
Selecting a Mann-Whitney U Test for non-parametric independent samples is well justified, reflecting appropriate statistical reasoning.
Include an effect-size measure (e.g., $$r$$) alongside the p-value to provide insight into the magnitude of the anchoring effect.
Discussion effectively links findings to Dual Process Theory, but could be enriched by acknowledging alternative cognitive biases influencing numerical estimates.
While modifications are proposed, explicitly link each suggestion (e.g., matched-pairs design) to the corresponding limitation to strengthen justification.
Summarize the purpose of sharing raw data in Appendix C by noting key patterns or outliers to guide readers before they view the appendix.
The caption describing the figure lacks a figure number. Add âFigure 1:â before the title to conform to standard presentation.
Identifying the mathematics-ability confound and proposing a matched-pairs design shows insightful evaluation and justified modification.
At the start of the Introduction, explicitly state the investigation aim and its relevance to anchoring bias theory to orient readers immediately.
